title: tulipa humilis var. crispatula
intro: Tulipa humilis var. crispatula, also known as the miniature or dwarf wild tulip, is a low-growing perennial plant belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Tulipa humilis var. crispatula include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as reduce inflammation and fever. This plant is typically prepared in herbal remedies through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
